retrieved_docs.title: Apical Periodontitis -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Apical diseases can manifest with various clinical presentations, ranging from no obvious clinical signs or symptoms to severe destruction of the underlying bone, with or without a draining abscess. Therefore, proper identification, diagnosis, and treatment of apical diseases can prevent severe clinical signs and symptoms.

retrieved_docs.title: Oral Surgery, Instruments -- Function -- Periotomes and Luxators
retrieved_docs.content: Where the root apex fractures within the socket, a root pick elevator is useful in luxating the apical tip. Where the surgeon struggles to find a definitive application point, bone removal at the level of the apical root tip may be required to improve access. These fine instruments have a posterior curve of the working end, allowing for successful application and luxation. [9]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Materials: Biodentine, a Calcium Silicate Bioactive -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Biodentine has emerged in recent years as an innovative bioactive dentine substitute. Its superior physical properties, improved handling, and various clinical applications have made biodentine popular in dentistry. It has been advocated for vital pulp therapy, root perforations, resorptions, retrograde fillings, and dentin replacement. Biodentine is calcium silicate-based cement formed by highly purified tricalcium silicate (80.1%). [1] Unlike mineral trioxide aggregate (MTA), it does not contain bismuth oxide, which is known to delay setting, affecting biocompatibility and causing discoloration. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Technique -- Access Cavity Basic Principles
retrieved_docs.content: Attention must also focus on replacing defective restorations. Faulty fillings compromise the coronal seal, limiting the effectiveness of endodontic therapy; replacement ensures proper closure of the access cavity. [47] [50] Finally, unsupported tooth tissue should be removed, as such remnants may compromise the seal and produce inaccuracies in determining the true length of the tooth. [47] [51] Careful adherence to these principles promotes predictable canal access, effective debridement, and long-term treatment success.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: The TrussAC is recommended when maintaining structural integrity is paramount, yet entry into multiple canals is required. This approach is most applicable in situations where canal orientations allow separate, small entry points and where restorative longevity and functional durability are key considerations.

retrieved_docs.title: Oral Surgery, Extraction of Teeth -- Contraindications -- Proximity to Vital Structures
retrieved_docs.content: Proximity to the antral sinuses can be a reason for caution when planning an extraction due to the potential to create an oroantral communication (OAC). However, if a tooth is acutely symptomatic, a high risk of an OAC may not be a sufficient reason to avoid extraction. Radiographic signs that a root apex sits in the sinus include deviation of the sinus floor, a lack of continuity of the sinus floor, and long roots visually invading the antral space. A clear consent process with the patient is key in these scenarios.

retrieved_docs.title: Apical Periodontitis -- Deterrence and Patient Education
retrieved_docs.content: Regular and comprehensive dental examinations are critical in the early identification of any infectious or carious process throughout the dentition. Therefore, dentists must emphasize to their patients how crucial frequent, comprehensive dental examinations are. In addition, patients who have received endodontic therapy in the past should be routinely evaluated clinically and radiographically to ensure the healing of their infections is progressing normally.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: The ConsAC is best suited for vital or minimally compromised anterior teeth, where preserving pericervical dentin is a priority. ConsAC is typically chosen for teeth with straightforward canal anatomy and minimal calcification, particularly when long-term fracture resistance is a central treatment goal.

retrieved_docs.title: Cracked Tooth Syndrome -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: Lee et al reported a 91% pulp survival rate in cracked teeth stabilized with bidirectional splinting, while Guthrie et al reported an 11% failure rate in crowned cracked teeth necessitating endodontic therapy. [27] [30] Tan et al.'s study indicated that teeth with extensive cracks reaching the pulp, necessitating endodontic therapy and definitive cuspal coverage, exhibit a diminished prognosis and are prone to eventual failure, often resulting in extraction. [33]

retrieved_docs.title: Oral Surgery, Extraction of Teeth -- Indications -- Pulpal/Apical Pathology
retrieved_docs.content: A common sequela of dental caries is pulpal and periapical pathology. A dental extraction would be indicated for a tooth with pulpal, or apical pathology that cannot be resolved with endodontic treatment or that would not be restorable following the endodontic treatment. In some cases, a dental extraction is indicated when the patient chooses not to have endodontic treatment but would still like the tooth to be treated.

retrieved_docs.title: CT Instrumentation and Physics -- Definition/Introduction -- Summary and additional points to consider
retrieved_docs.content: Pitch is the ratio of the distance the table moved in a single x-ray tube rotation divided by the beam width. A beam width of 1 will have no slice overlap. A pitch greater than 1 will have slice gaps, while a less than 1 will overlap. [22]

retrieved_docs.title: Trousseau Sign -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Calcium is an essential mineral that supports key cellular functions, particularly in the nervous, cardiovascular, and musculoskeletal systems. [1] This mineral acts as a secondary messenger in intracellular signal transduction pathways and plays a critical role in action potential generation, neurotransmitter release, and enzyme activity. [2] Most of the calcium in the body is located in bones and teeth, whereas 1% to 2% circulates in serum, where it is primarily bound to serum proteins and other anions. [3] Severe derangements in calcium homeostasis can result in life-threatening pathology. [1] Prompt identification of such abnormalities through a detailed history, a physical examination, and laboratory testing facilitates timely patient treatment.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Classification
retrieved_docs.content: Selection of an access cavity should strike a balance between structural preservation and adequate canal debridement. Classification of both the pulp chamber and access cavity design enables endodontists to tailor their approach to individual tooth anatomy, thereby reducing procedural complications while maintaining long-term tooth strength.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Classification -- Root Canal Sealers
retrieved_docs.content: Properties of an ideal sealer material: Ability to establish a fluid-tight seal Radiopaque (to be visible on radiographs) Antimicrobial Non-shrinking upon setting Non-staining Non-irritating to periapical tissues Insoluble in tissue fluid Biocompatible [1] [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Contraindications
retrieved_docs.content: There are few true contraindications for sealers and core obturation materials. One major contraindication is an allergy to any component of the material. [35] Allergies to constituent materials are rare but possible in every sealer and core material discussed, and the operator should take care to review all of a patient's possible allergies and be able to respond should a medical emergency arise. Other non-absolute contraindications are case-specific and depend on the treatment goal of the operator.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 2527
retrieved_docs.content: The FDA Center for Devices and Radiological Health Terminology includes the medical concepts of إجهاض فائت (missed abortion) and العقم (infertility) within its subset, indicating that these reproductive health conditions are recognized and standardized in the FDA’s terminology framework for devices and radiological health.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 1497
retrieved_docs.content: The FDA Center for Devices and Radiological Health Terminology includes the reproductive health concepts إجهاض فائت (missed abortion) and العقم (infertility) within its defined subset of terms. In this framework, إجهاض فائت is linked to ولادة جنين ميت (birth of a dead fetus), reflecting the clinical relationship between a fetal death that remains in utero and the eventual delivery of a non‑viable infant. Recognizing both إجهاض فائت and العقم in the FDA terminology underscores their importance for device regulation, diagnostic classification, and the management of patients experiencing these reproductive disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 2 -- Community 889
retrieved_docs.content: “High risk” status is also linked to cesarean section. Preterm labor is a child concept of the broader disorder اضطراب الحمل (pregnancy disorder). It is clinically associated with “High risk”, with poor reproductive history, with optional termination of pregnancy, and with the cesarean‑section scar type unknown. The FDA Center for Devices and Radiological Health Terminology includes a wide range of reproductive‑health concepts such as preterm labor, missed abortion (إجهاض فائت), infertility (العقم), tuberculous cavity infection (عدوى في الجوف السلوي), and optional termination of pregnancy. The code “HAURGALTZEA, ERAGINDAKOA” classifies induced abortion, is recorded as a value for the pregnancy‑outcome property, and is synonymous with الإجهاض المحرض (induced abortion). High‑risk status is also clinically associated with vaginal bleeding (نزيف مهبلي). Vaginal bleeding is a manifestation of the non‑inflammatory disorder of the female genital organs and serves as a marker of high risk.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2391
retrieved_docs.content: Abortion, also described as termination of pregnancy, is classified under the term الإجهاض المحرض, which denotes an induced abortion.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 132
retrieved_docs.content: phrase إجْهاضٌ مَصْحُوبٌ بعَدْوَى (abortion accompanied by infection). Additionally, female infertility is conceptually linked to the Arabic term غِيَابُ الإِباضَة (amenorrhea), highlighting that an absence of menstruation may underlie infertility. Together, these interconnected concepts illustrate how umbilical cord complications, operative vaginal deliveries, obstetric trauma, female pelvic pathologies, menstrual dysfunction, and post‑abortion infectious sequelae are interrelated through shared anatomical sites, classification hierarchies, and procedural contexts, providing a comprehensive framework for clinical assessment and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 3 -- Community 2500
retrieved_docs.content: Abortion (termination of pregnancy) is classified as a specific instance of إنهاء الحمل (جراحي), indicating that it falls under the broader category of surgical termination of pregnancy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 1850
retrieved_docs.content: Abdominal pregnancy (حمل منتبذ) is clinically associated with laparotomy (شق البطن), because surgical entry into the abdominal cavity is frequently required for the diagnosis, evaluation, and definitive management of this rare ectopic gestation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 2 -- Community 777
retrieved_docs.content: and uterine scars from previous surgeries, including previous cesarean sections, are specific indications that belong to the larger group of maternal‑care indications triggered by known or suspected pelvic‑organ abnormalities. These indications guide clinical decision‑making throughout pregnancy, labor, and delivery, and they are consistently represented across Dutch and Arabic terminology, ensuring unified understanding and management of maternal health risks.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 3 -- Community 2061
retrieved_docs.content: Perinatal Terminology encompasses a broad spectrum of obstetric concepts and conditions that are essential for precise communication in maternal‑fetal medicine. It includes the term “vaginální porod po císařském řezu,” which denotes a vaginal birth after a previous cesarean section, a delivery option that requires careful assessment of uterine scar integrity. The Arabic concept “انْصِمامٌ بالسَّائِلِ السَّلَوِيّ” describes fluid embolism, a rare but potentially fatal event in which embolic material enters the circulation, demanding rapid recognition and intervention. “حمل بطني” refers to an abdominal pregnancy, a form of ectopic gestation where the embryo implants within the abdominal cavity, posing significant risks to both mother and fetus. “حمل مطول” indicates a prolonged or post‑term pregnancy, a condition associated with increased perinatal morbidity that often prompts consideration of induction or delivery planning. The phrase “رحى عدارية خبيثة” identifies a malignant uterine fibroid (or malignant uterine tumor), a neoplastic growth that can complicate pregnancy by causing obstruction, bleeding, or invasion of surrounding structures. “مشيمة ملتصقة” (placenta accreta) describes abnormal adherence of the placenta to the uterine wall, a serious condition that can lead to massive hemorrhage during delivery and frequently necessitates surgical management. Finally, “ولادة مهبلية” simply denotes vaginal delivery, the physiological process of giving birth through the birth canal. Collectively, these terms illustrate the diverse range of normal and pathological scenarios captured within Perinatal Terminology, providing clinicians with a comprehensive lexical framework for diagnosis, risk stratification, and optimal patient care in the perinatal period.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 1927
retrieved_docs.content: The FDA Center for Devices and Radiological Health Terminology includes the concepts إجهاض فائت (missed abortion) and العقم (infertility) within its subset of medical terms. In the context of إجهاض فائت, the associated finding site is identified as plodové struktury (fetal structures), indicating that the loss or abnormality pertains to the developing fetal anatomy. By categorizing both إجهاض فائت and العقم, the FDA terminology highlights their importance for device regulation, diagnostic criteria, and patient‑care considerations in reproductive health.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 3 -- Community 2401
retrieved_docs.content: Previous c‑section and maternal care for other types of scar from a prior cesarean delivery are both classified under the term “عملية قيصرية سابقة,” which denotes a previous cesarean operation. This classification groups the English concepts of a prior c‑section and related maternal scar‑care together with the Arabic label, highlighting their shared relevance in obstetric history and maternal care planning.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2417
retrieved_docs.content: Infectious process is identified as a pathological mechanism underlying post‑abortion complications, and these complications are referred to in Arabic as “إجْهاضٌ مَصْحُوبٌ بعَدْوَى.” This relationship highlights that infection can be a key contributor to adverse outcomes following an abortion, emphasizing the importance of recognizing and managing infectious etiologies to prevent or treat post‑abortion complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 4 -- Community 2846
retrieved_docs.content: The concept of an allowed qualifier is applied to the prevention and control of mitral valve stenosis, threatened abortion, and fetal distress syndrome. Preventive and control strategies for mitral valve stenosis aim to limit valve narrowing and reduce cardiac complications. In cases of threatened abortion, early identification and appropriate interventions are essential to prevent progression to miscarriage and to protect maternal and fetal health. For fetal distress syndrome, vigilant monitoring and timely therapeutic measures are crucial to manage fetal compromise and improve outcomes for both mother and child. Integrating prevention and control measures for these conditions supports better patient care and reduces the risk of adverse events.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2338
retrieved_docs.content: Abortion (termination of pregnancy) is classified as الإجهاض المحرض and is regarded as a subcategory under إنهاء الحمل (جراحي), indicating that the broader concept of surgical termination of pregnancy encompasses the specific type of induced abortion.

Perinatal Terminology aggregates these procedures with related clinical entities such as anterior and posterior asynclitism, malposition, disproportion, failure to induce labor, prolonged first‑stage labor, prolonged second‑stage labor, secondary uterine inertia, uterine hypotonia, uterine atony, adhesive placenta, premature placental separation, amniotic fluid embolism, prolonged pregnancy, ovarian pregnancy, abdominal pregnancy, reduction of multiple pregnancy, and other placental disorders. Female reproductive function connects functional assessments (uterine hypotonia, secondary uterine inertia) to infertility evaluation, dyspareunia of non‑psychogenic origin, amenorrhea, and post‑abortion complications,
